reflection.candidate_lesson: When the 30-indicator consensus shows high conviction (>90%) but green_matches=0 and hist_hr=0%, treat the signal as untested rather than strong — conviction without historical confirmation is a size-down condition, not a trade trigger.
reflection.mechanism: Conviction measures current indicator agreement; green_matches/hist_hr measure whether that exact configuration has ever profited historically. AUDNZD shows 92% conviction SHORT but zero historical matches — this is a novel indicator alignment the library has never seen resolve. Institutional flow that produces durable moves leaves repeated footprints; a config with no historical analog is more likely a transient crowding artifact than a persistent edge, and sizing it as high-conviction imports variance without expected return.
reflection.testable_rule: if consensus_conviction > 0.85 and green_matches == 0 and hist_hr == 0.0: size_multiplier = 0.5  # untested-config penalty until >=1 historical match exists

validation.reason: Rule filters only 0 trades — insufficient sample
